sections.heading: What's a queue?
sections.body: A **queue** is like a stack's polite twin. You **enqueue** items at the back. You **dequeue** them from the front. First in, first out — **FIFO**.

sections.title: The grocery line
sections.body: You walk into the store, see five people ahead of you, and join the back. When the cashier is ready, the person at the front goes — not you. Everyone moves up. That's a queue.

sections.caption: Enter at the back. Leave from the front.

sections.heading: Where queues show up
sections.body: Print jobs. Customer support tickets. Tasks waiting for a CPU. Most importantly for DSA: **BFS (breadth-first search)** on trees and graphs uses a queue to explore level by level.

questions.question: Queue ordering is:
questions.explanation: FIFO = First In, First Out. The earliest arrival leaves first.
